defends: New-device / new-sign-in alerting on the apex Google and GitHub identities: an attacker authenticating from an unrecognised device raises a notification the owner can act on, giving early detection of account takeover (T1078 valid accounts).

state_document.google_new_device_alerts_enabled: Google account: new-device / unusual sign-in security alerts are enabled
state_document.github_new_device_alerts_enabled: GitHub account: new-device sign-in (and new-device email) alerts are enabled

live_target.source: Google account-security settings + GitHub account-security/notification settings (respective account-security APIs)
live_target.note: unknown without live creds; use --state-fixture offline

self_guard: bad fixture sets github_new_device_alerts_enabled=false (disabled control injected); validator must flag fail (negative control fires). good fixture has both keys true and must pass clean.
note: Fixed evidence manifest — the audited identities and the required state keys are not model discretion (WORKING_METHOD §1). All required keys must be true to pass; any false fails; any missing/unreadable is unknown.
